title: LP Course Authoring Format (v1)
description: The SIMPLE, content-only format. An LLM formats raw text into this; the compiler expands it into the playable lp_lesson_items state machine. This format intentionally has NO button pointers, NO uuids, NO menu/feedback wiring — the compiler adds all of that deterministically. You author meaning; code owns structure.

voice.description: Optional teaching voice/stance injected into feedback + Ask, e.g. 'warm, plain-spoken, faith-informed (DUO/Sage)'. Lets the tutor sound like the creator.

destination.description: What the student can DO by the end. The arc points here. e.g. 'has a missed-call automation spec ready for MightyMax to build'.

lessons.description: The ARC — order matters, this is the spine. Each lesson is one teachable step toward the destination.

source_text.description: The raw lesson body. Used for Ask retrieval (chunked + embedded) and as grounding for generation. The student never sees this as a page.

concepts.description: 3-6 teachable concepts. Each becomes a concept_tag for mastery tracking.

quizzes.description: Quiz SEEDS — content only. Compiler wires them into the state machine with feedback steps + pointers.

concept.description: must match a concepts[].tag in this lesson

feedback.description: what the bot says when this option is tapped

scenarios.description: Role-play SEEDS. One setup, 2-3 branch choices, each with a consequence + coaching.

setup.description: the in-character situation the bot presents

flashcards.description: Front prompt + back reveal. Compiler makes these flip steps.
